stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (solar panels) yields a guaranteed return of $198K. Option B (wind turbines) depends on weather patterns: with probability 39-63% (uncertain), it yields $487K; otherwise it yields $90K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in weather patterns affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $198K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $244.8K to $340.1K. Point estimate: $292.5K. Option B is preferred at most assumptions (EV_B > EV_A when P(good) > 27.2%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 27.2%, which falls outside the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
